stories.title: Vous prévoyez d'aller voir l'éclipse solaire le 12 août ? Racontez-nous comment vous vous êtes organisé
stories.summary: Mercredi 12 août 2026, une éclipse solaire totale traversera l’hémisphère Nord, passant par la Russie, le Groenland, l’Islande et l’Espagne, où elle sera visible en totalité. En France, le phénomène sera partiellement observable, avec jusqu’à 94,9 % du Soleil masqué à Guéret. Le maximum de l’éclipse aura lieu en soirée, près de l’horizon, offrant un spectacle lumineux unique. Les observateurs devront utiliser des lunettes adaptées pour une vision sécurisée.

stories.title: PRONOS PARIS RMC Le pari sûr du 22 juillet - Ligue Conférence
stories.summary: SC Braga faces FK Železničar Pančevo in the UEFA Europa Conference League second qualifying round on July 22 at 21:00 CET. Braga, unbeaten in five pre-season matches, starts as heavy favorite after opening their domestic league with a win. Železničar, fourth in Serbia’s 2025-26 league, gains an early-season edge with a competitive start. Braga is expected to advance despite Serbian preparation gains.

stories.title: Morant: "Feijóo está dibujando una coalición que se parece cada vez más a esos gobiernos criminales como el de Hitler"
stories.summary: Spain’s science minister Diana Morant accused PP leader Alberto Núñez Feijóo of steering a potential coalition toward policies resembling Nazi Germany, citing rising xenophobic rhetoric and racist attacks on footballer Lamine Yamal. The PP demanded an apology, calling her comparison unacceptable. Morant warned of laws targeting citizens and urged the PP to condemn hate speech. The dispute escalated after her TVE remarks on Wednesday.

stories.title: Charli XCX recrute Vincent Cassel dans le clip de son nouveau single "Camera"
stories.summary: Charli XCX’s new single Camera, from her album Music, Fashion, Film out July 24, features an unexpected cameo by Vincent Cassel in its music video. Filmed in black and white, the clip shows Cassel attempting a desert shoot scene that repeatedly fails due to technical mishaps, including a misfiring explosive and a flaming projector. Frustrated, he mimics a gun to his temple—a nod to his iconic role in La Haine—before storming off set. The clip blends high-energy pop with cinematic chaos ahead of the album’s release.

stories.title: Villa agree loan deal for Chelsea winger Garnacho
stories.summary: Aston Villa have agreed a loan deal for Chelsea winger Alejandro Garnacho until the end of the 2026-27 season, with an obligation to buy. The 22-year-old Argentina international has completed a medical in Birmingham and has a pre-agreed four-year contract at Villa Park if the loan becomes permanent. The obligation to buy clause is expected to be triggered based on appearances next season. Villa have made four signings this summer, while Chelsea have recouped nearly £200m from player sales.

stories.title: Meteo MARCHE Video: previsioni aggiornate
stories.summary: Heavy rain and localized thunderstorms hit the Marche region Friday afternoon, prompting a 36-hour yellow weather alert from 2 p.m. Friday to midnight Sunday. The worst-hit areas included the Pesarese sector, with towns like Piandimeleto, Mercatale and Peglio reporting intense downpours. Brief coastal and hillside showers are expected overnight Saturday into Sunday morning before conditions stabilize with dry, sunny weather through Monday.

stories.title: ‘Se você quiser, conversaremos sobre tudo o que houve’: como é a relação com a filha de Elize Matsunaga
stories.summary: A filha de Elize Matsunaga, Helena, de 16 anos, vive com os avós paternos desde o assassinato de seu pai, Marcos Kitano Matsunaga, em 2012. A Justiça mantém Elize sem autorização para visitas ou comunicação direta com a filha. Em 2021, durante uma saída temporária, Elize exibiu um cartaz à adolescente com a mensagem "Minha filha, te amarei além da vida". A relação entre mãe e filha poderá ser reavaliada quando Helena atingir a maioridade.

stories.title: Televisión Canaria presenta ‘Venezuela: un mes bajo los escombros’
stories.summary: Televisión Canaria presents a special report tonight at 21:30 analyzing the humanitarian crisis after Venezuela’s double earthquake on June 24. The disaster killed over 5,000 people, injured thousands, and destroyed or severely damaged nearly 18,000 homes. The program marks one month since the quake, focusing on recovery efforts in La Guaira and Caracas. A local team documented the emergency on the ground over the past weeks.

stories.title: Accidental death recorded at inquest of Kerry teenager
stories.summary: A jury has returned a verdict of accidental death for 14-year-old Thomas Healy, who died when the car he was driving crashed into a tree on Ross Road, Killarney, on July 7, 2021. The car was being pursued by a Garda van after leaving a Killarney shopping centre car park. His 15-year-old passenger, who was severely injured, told the inquest they had been trying to calm each other before the crash. A 2022 Garda investigation cleared the pursuing officer of any wrongdoing.

stories.title: Islande: vague d'indignation dans le pays après le saccage d'un site naturel par un touriste français
stories.summary: A French tourist destroyed protected landscapes in Iceland’s central Highlands in June by driving two Land Rovers off-route, posting videos online. Local authorities fined him 300,000 ISK (about €3,000), but he continued the damage. Police later arrested him again for the same offense. Iceland’s government faces growing criticism for its response to the incident.

stories.title: He spotted a sports relic in the thrift store. It’s about to make him rich.
stories.summary: A 59-year-old hiker died after being struck by lightning on Utah’s American Fork Twin Peaks Saturday. Craig Nelson collapsed near the summit while descending with his brother as storm clouds rolled in. Rescue teams waited for the storm to pass before retrieving his body. Authorities continue investigating while awaiting autopsy results.

stories.title: Il processo di stasera a "Un giorno in pretura"
stories.summary: The trial of Giampiero Gualandi, accused of killing Sofia Stefani, concludes tonight on Un giorno in pretura at 9:15 PM on Rai 3. The 64-year-old former police commander allegedly shot the 33-year-old vigilessa in Anzola dell’Emilia on May 16, 2024, over a volatile relationship. Prosecutors claim he feared exposure of their affair, while the defense argues Stefani provoked the fatal shooting. The case centers on disputed accounts of their troubled romance.

stories.title: Cameron Menzies became unwell during his second-round match against Ross Smith
stories.summary: Cameron Menzies’ World Matchplay darts match against Ross Smith was abandoned Wednesday night after he became dizzy and fainted at Blackpool’s Winter Gardens. The 37-year-old Scot, leading 2-1, was treated for high blood pressure and later returned to his accommodation. The second-round clash will not resume. Later sessions feature Gian van Veen and Gerwyn Price as they vie for quarter-final spots.

stories.title: Ola de calor en España: ahora llega el punto álgido con hasta 44ºC
stories.summary: Spain braces for peak heatwave temperatures of up to 44°C between Wednesday and Thursday, with red alerts issued for Murcia, Alicante and Valencia. The interior, Andalusia and Madrid will see widespread highs above 40°C, while Mediterranean regions get slight relief from coastal breezes. Forecasters warn the heat will intensify before easing over the weekend.

stories.title: Gratteri bacchetta Salvini sul caso Roggero: “La politica si dia una calmata e la smetta di aizzare la gente”. Su La7
stories.summary: Nicola Gratteri, procuratore capo di Napoli, ha criticato duramente la politica per aver strumentalizzato due casi giudiziari: gli scontri di piazza a Bologna e la condanna del gioielliere Mario Roggero. Gratteri ha definito le polemiche "propaganda elettorale" e ha invitato a rispettare le sentenze definitive, sottolineando che la grazia può essere concessa solo dopo un percorso di ravvedimento in carcere. Ha esortato a evitare processi mediatici, affidando la valutazione ai magistrati e agli educatori penitenziari.

stories.title: Watch Maidenhead friendly on Wolves TV | Men's First-Team | News
stories.summary: Wolves face Maidenhead United in their first pre-season friendly under new head coach Cesar Peixoto this Wednesday, July 22, at York Road. The National League South side hosts the Premier League club in a landmark match tied to a past transfer deal. Fans can watch the 7.30pm kickoff for free on Wolves TV with full commentary. The fixture is part of a four-game pre-season schedule, with three subsequent matches priced at £5 each.

stories.title: La Stazione spaziale internazionale attraversa i cieli: passaggio visibile anche nel Bresciano. Ecco quando
stories.summary: Tonight, July 18, the International Space Station will be visible over Brescia at 11:08 PM. The five-minute pass will appear as a bright, steady light moving east-northeast. No equipment is needed if skies are clear. Check Heavens-Above for exact timing by location.

stories.title: [Galaxy Unpacked July 2026] A First Look at Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra, Galaxy Z Fold8 and Galaxy Z Flip8
stories.summary: Samsung unveiled three new foldable smartphones at Galaxy Unpacked in London on July 2026. The lineup includes the first Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra with an 8-inch foldable display and a 4.1mm thin profile when unfolded. The series also features the Galaxy Z Fold8 and Galaxy Z Flip8, both enhanced with AI-driven features. Shipments are expected to begin next month.

stories.title: Price-hiked iPads are a little cheaper right now
stories.summary: A premium 13-inch iPad Pro with 512GB of storage is currently discounted by $150 at Amazon and $100 at Best Buy, bringing its price down to $1,549.99. This high-end iPad features Apple's M5 processor, an OLED screen, and support for the Apple Pencil Pro and Magic Keyboard. The deal is available on the 2025 model, which normally costs $1,699. Those looking for a more affordable option may also consider the discounted 11-inch iPad Air M3 with 1TB of storage, now priced at $749.

stories.title: EastEnders kicks off Linda Carter romance story as new character arrives
stories.summary: A new romance is set to unfold in EastEnders as Linda Carter sparks up a connection with councillor Clive Masters, played by Jason Durr. The pair’s chemistry unfolds during a salsa class at Walford’s community centre, where Linda rallies locals to save the venue. Scenes airing next week show their instant attraction after Ian arranges a meeting. The storyline follows Linda’s fight to keep the centre open, bringing Clive into her orbit.

stories.title: Gespierde armen, krachtige taal: Van Persie en Steijn beloven 'heel veel goals'
stories.summary: Feyenoord’s training camp in Belgium features 32 players, including returning striker Shaqueel van Persie and Sem Steijn, both recovering from knee injuries. Van Persie aims to reclaim the starting striker role and score consistently under new coach Giovanni van Bronckhorst. Steijn, also injury-hit, highlights off-field conditioning as key to his comeback. The squad’s physical focus signals high expectations for the upcoming season.

stories.title: ‘Disney Worldbuilders’ Documentary by Leslie Iwerks, Featuring James Cameron, Kevin Feige and Jon Favreau, Set to Premiere on Disney+
stories.summary: A new documentary, Disney Worldbuilders, premieres on Disney+ August 20, offering a behind-the-scenes look at how Disney turns films like Frozen and Zootopia into theme park attractions. Directed by Leslie Iwerks, the film features interviews with James Cameron, Kevin Feige, and Jon Favreau. The documentary traces the creative process from screen to park, showcasing the collaboration behind immersive Disney worlds. Viewers can expect exclusive insights into the making of these attractions.

stories.title: ‘EA Sports FC’ Sets World Cup Recordbreaker Kylian Mbappé as New Cover Athlete (Gaming News Roundup)
stories.summary: Kylian Mbappé will grace the cover of EA Sports FC 27’s Ultimate Edition and mobile version, marking his first cover appearance since 2020. The French striker, now at Real Madrid, set a World Cup goals record with his 22nd goal in the latest tournament. EA Sports calls him a defining player of his generation. The game launches this fall.

stories.title: Burnham stirs up political row with review of release plans for violent criminals
stories.summary: Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham has ordered a review of plans to release thousands of violent offenders from prison early, affecting around 6,000 individuals. The move comes after the family of PC Andrew Harper expressed outrage over the potential release of his killers. The review aims to address concerns over public safety and the criminal justice system. The outcome of the review will likely influence the government's decision on the early release of these offenders.

stories.title: iOS code could reportedly let Apple cut off apps when users miss iPhone payments
stories.summary: Apple's iOS 27 beta code reveals a new "Restricted Mode" that could be triggered if users miss iPhone payments, restricting access to nearly all apps except for basic functions. This feature is part of a new financing program, "Apple Upgrade," which allows users to lease new devices. The code also includes a "Partner Finance Lock" to prevent device erasure or resale. If implemented, this feature would impact users who fail to make payments on financed iPhones.

stories.title: €1.5 billion for tax cuts in Budget - Economic Statement
stories.summary: €1.5 billion in tax cuts is planned for October’s Budget, as outlined in the Summer Economic Statement. The Government also proposes €7 billion in spending increases next year, including €5.9 billion for day-to-day costs and €1.1 billion for infrastructure like energy and transport. Total expenditure will rise to €125 billion in 2027, a 5.9% increase on 2026. The statement warns of caution over reliance on corporation tax revenue.

stories.title: Bose Wants to Be the Centerpiece of Your Living Room Again With the Lifestyle Ultra Speaker
stories.summary: Bose has relaunched its Lifestyle Ultra Speaker, priced at $299, as the flagship wireless speaker in its revived Lifestyle Collection. The new model, available in Black and White Smoke, is a bestseller on Best Buy and joins the Lifestyle Ultra Soundbar and Subwoofer. Customers can start with one speaker and expand into a multi-room setup or full home theater. A Best Buy bundle with the soundbar and subwoofer is offered at $1,699.

stories.title: Toter Wal vom Badestrand entfernt - Teile des Kopfes fehlten
stories.summary: A dead porpoise washed ashore on Norderney’s popular beach was removed Tuesday after parts of its head were missing. Authorities confirmed the carcass was taken to disposal following reports Monday near a tourist bar. A second dead porpoise found two weeks ago was sent for examination on the mainland. Inspectors could not analyze the latest remains due to severe decomposition.

stories.title: Teenager drops social media addiction lawsuit against Meta
stories.summary: A 15-year-old Florida boy has dropped his lawsuit against Meta, citing concerns about a lengthy jury trial, after previously settling similar claims against TikTok, Snapchat, and YouTube. The case, which aimed to hold social media companies accountable for allegedly creating addictive platforms, is now closed. Meta claims the lawsuit was baseless. The teenager will focus on his recovery and therapy.

stories.title: Police formally investigate woman after Brit stabbed to death in French village
stories.summary: A 70-year-old woman has been formally investigated in connection with the 2023 stabbing death of 65-year-old British-South African mother Karen Carter in Trémolat, France. Carter was found with stab wounds near her car by a friend in the Dordogne village where she lived and managed two holiday rental homes. The woman was initially taken into custody in 2025 as part of preliminary inquiries before being released. She will now face a trial decision after the investigating judge found sufficient evidence.

stories.title: Trump threatens to target Iran's bridges and power plants if Hormuz attacks persist
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has threatened to destroy Iranian bridges and power plants if attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz continue. The US has launched strikes on Iran for the 11th consecutive night, prompting Tehran to activate air defences in the capital. Trump's threat specifically targets structures located next to or in Tehran. Iran has warned of reprisals after the US said it would soon hit an underground nuclear site near Natanz.

stories.title: Nie żyje piosenkarz Ryszard Pisarski. "Wielki smutek i szok"
stories.summary: Popularny w czasach PRL piosenkarz Ryszard Pisarski zmarł 21 lipca 2026 roku w wieku 87 lat. Informację o jego śmierci podał dziennikarz Maciej Klociński, który dzień wcześniej rozmawiał z artystą. Przyczyna zgonu nie została ujawniona. Rodzina i fani składają kondolencje.

stories.title: Tennis: Ana Ivanović macht ein trauriges Geständnis
stories.summary: Ana Ivanović revealed she retired from tennis in December 2016 due to severe mental health struggles, including panic attacks during matches. The former French Open champion and world No. 1 spoke openly about her decision for the first time in a July 2026 interview. Ivanović, now 38, cited injuries and mental health as key factors in her early exit at age 29. She described debilitating panic attacks triggered by stepping onto the court.

stories.title: The Hawk review – Will Ferrell’s dated golf comedy just isn’t that funny
stories.summary: Will Ferrell stars in a new Netflix golf comedy, his first scripted series since 2019. The show premiered on April 18, 2025, marking his return to broad comedy after recent dramatic roles. Reviews call it a throwback to early 2000s slapstick, testing whether Ferrell’s style still resonates. Critics note the series leans into the same brand of exaggerated humor that defined his breakout films.

stories.title: Tories ask HMRC to investigate whether Nigel Farage owes tax on £5m gift
stories.summary: The Conservatives have asked HMRC to investigate whether Nigel Farage owes tax on a £5m gift from crypto billionaire Christopher Harborne. Farage claims the money was a personal gift with no conditions, while the Tories suggest it may have been compensation for political activities. HMRC has not yet confirmed if an investigation will proceed. Farage has previously stated the funds were for security and Brexit campaigning.

stories.title: NASA to Showcase Agency’s Newest Wind Tunnel in Virginia
stories.summary: NASA will showcase its newest wind tunnel, the Flight Dynamics Research Facility, at the Langley Research Center in Hampton, Virginia, on July 31. The facility, the agency's first new wind tunnel in over 40 years, will be open to a media tour and ribbon-cutting ceremony. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman and other officials will be available for media questions. The event marks a significant milestone in NASA's evolution and will provide insight into the agency's future research capabilities.

stories.title: Meldungen - Schlag gegen eine der weltweit gefährlichsten Phishing-Gruppierungen
stories.summary: German and U.S. authorities dismantled the Kratos phishing service on July 20, 2026, seizing over 200 servers and arresting the Indonesian developer. The tool helped criminals create fake Microsoft login pages to steal credentials from 850 victims across 35 countries. Authorities say Kratos operated as a malware-as-a-service platform, rented to other cybercriminals. Investigations continue into the broader network of users.

stories.title: „Mir wurde die Hose runtergezogen und zwischen die Beine gefasst“
stories.summary: Bill Kaulitz, 36, frontman of Tokio Hotel, revealed he faced queerphobic assaults as a teenager, including having his pants pulled down and being groped in a swimming pool. The attacks aimed to question his gender identity, with Kaulitz calling the incidents nearly routine and keeping them secret out of shame. In his 2021 memoir, he first documented these experiences. Kaulitz now advocates for greater acceptance in the music industry.

stories.title: The Odyssey film fans inspired to go back to the source
stories.summary: The Odyssey film grossed £12.6m in the UK, making it the second-biggest opening weekend of the year behind Toy Story 5. Shot entirely with Imax 70mm cameras, it set a UK record for highest Imax opening weekend takings. Only three UK screens—London’s BFI and Science Museum, and Manchester’s Printworks—are showing it in 70mm. Screenings cost more but critics call the experience immersive.

stories.title: Après les fortes chaleurs, des orages possiblement violents sont attendus… mais jusqu’à quand va durer la canicule dans la région
stories.summary: A heatwave gripping southern France’s Languedoc-Roussillon region will persist until Saturday, with temperatures still exceeding 30°C daily. Storms, potentially severe, are forecast to arrive Saturday as conditions shift. Mistral winds are currently driving the heat, but a weather change is expected by the weekend.

stories.title: Catherine Keyl schrikt van bizarre tips Tim Hofman: ‘Dit is shocking’
stories.summary: Catherine Keyl expressed shock over controversial homework tips in a book by Tim Hofman for the BrainBoost tutoring school. The book allegedly includes advice on cheating, such as using multiple phones to look up answers during tests. Hofman’s publisher confirmed the book’s release, though details remain under scrutiny. Authorities may review the content following public backlash.

stories.title: Rangers to fight McInnes ban for 'disgusting' penalty remark
stories.summary: Rangers manager Derek McInnes has been given a four-match touchline ban by the Scottish FA for calling a late penalty decision "disgusting". The penalty, awarded to Celtic in a 3-2 win at Motherwell in May, was a stoppage-time spot-kick that kept Celtic one point behind Hearts. McInnes is contesting the ban, claiming his comments were directed at the decision, not officials. He will serve the first three games of the ban, with the fourth suspended until the end of the season.

stories.title: Modelling scout linked to Epstein found dead in Paris
stories.summary: A modelling scout linked to Jeffrey Epstein was found dead at his Paris home on Monday. Daniel Siad, 69, was discovered in Colombes, a northwest suburb, and authorities opened an investigation into his death. Siad’s name appeared thousands of times in released Epstein files, though he denied knowledge of Epstein’s crimes. French prosecutors had previously investigated him for alleged human trafficking and conspiracy but closed the case in February due to insufficient evidence.

stories.title: Inter Miami under investigation after signing Casemiro
stories.summary: Major League Soccer is investigating Inter Miami for alleged tampering after the club signed Brazilian midfielder Casemiro on a deal through 2027 with an option to extend until 2029. The investigation concerns Casemiro's discovery rights, which Los Angeles Galaxy held before Inter Miami acquired them. The league is gathering information and will comment once the review is complete. A settlement between Inter Miami and the LA Galaxy for Casemiro's rights is pending the outcome of the investigation.

stories.title: NCIS Reveals First-Ever Recast Character in 31 Years in New Set Video
stories.summary: NCIS will recast a character for the first time in its 31-year history, as revealed in a new set video. The change comes as the long-running series prepares for its upcoming season. Details on the recast or affected character have not been disclosed. The shift marks a significant milestone for the iconic procedural.

stories.title: Dubai Firm Cuts Deal for New Port to Bypass Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: A Dubai-based logistics giant unveiled a deal with the United Arab Emirates on Wednesday to develop a new port system that would bypass the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: RETE 4
stories.summary: A 90-year-old Illinois florist turned drug courier, Earl Stone, faces prison after a 2016 bust. The film The Mule recounts his 2016-2018 run transporting cocaine for a cartel. Based on a true story, the movie premieres December 14. Eastwood stars as Stone, reflecting on family and regret.

stories.title: It's Officially the End of an Era for Marvel's 'Wonder Man'
stories.summary: It's 2024: Marvel's "Wonder Man" series has been canceled after one season. The show starred Yahya Abdul-Mateen II as the titular hero, set in the MCU. Production wrapped in late 2023. Fans can expect a finale resolving key storylines next year.

stories.title: Laredo’s Future Rides on Trump’s Next Trade Move
stories.summary: The economy of Laredo, Texas, has boomed under the president’s global tariffs. Now, his demands may threaten its success.

stories.title: Israeli Banks Threaten to Cut Off Palestinian Economy
stories.summary: Two Israeli financial firms have long served as partners to Palestinian banks, handling transactions that make possible the West Bank’s imports and exports.

stories.title: Hitler’s Birthplace in Austria Is Now a Police Station
stories.summary: The government repurposed the 17th century building in hopes of preventing the site from becoming an attraction for Nazi sympathizers.

stories.title: Sony Revives Cinerama Dome in Los Angeles as Box Office Sales Rebound
stories.summary: The iconic Cinerama Dome and adjacent 14-screen theater complex will reopen in 2028 after a renovation. The venue closed in 2020 because of the pandemic.

stories.title: Trump’s Trade Negotiator Doubles Down on Tariffs Before Global Duties Expire
stories.summary: Jamieson Greer, the president’s top trade negotiator, told the Senate that the United States remained intent on tariffs as it prepared to replace duties the Supreme Court struck down.

stories.title: Global Oil Price Jumps as War Threats Intensify
stories.summary: But stock investors shrug off Iran developments as they await crucial earnings reports from tech firms including Alphabet and Tesla.

stories.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
stories.summary: A British health committee report finds that ultra-processed foods contribute to obesity, with sugar and refined carbohydrates being primary drivers. The report recommends targeting these specific nutrients in food policy. The committee's findings are based on evidence showing that fat and salt are not the main culprits in weight gain and type 2 diabetes. The UK government is now under pressure to revise its food labeling and reformulation targets accordingly.

stories.title: Fifth person dies in New York City amid legionnaires’ disease outbreak
stories.summary: A fifth person has died in New York City’s legionnaires’ disease outbreak, bringing the toll to five fatalities among 82 confirmed cases. Health officials traced the outbreak to cooling towers on Manhattan’s Upper East Side, where 34 tested positive for legionella bacteria. Eight patients remain hospitalized, while 56 have recovered and been discharged. No new cases have been reported in six days, but officials warn figures may still change as testing continues.

stories.title: Government Overhauls Teen Pregnancy Program to Focus on Marriage and Starting Families
stories.summary: The administration canceled federal grants to organizations across the country, accusing them of encouraging premarital sex. New criteria say programs should stress abstinence.

offbeat.title: Vous prévoyez d'aller voir l'éclipse solaire le 12 août ? Racontez-nous comment vous vous êtes organisé
offbeat.summary: Mercredi 12 août 2026, une éclipse solaire totale traversera l’hémisphère Nord, passant par la Russie, le Groenland, l’Islande et l’Espagne, où elle sera visible en totalité. En France, le phénomène sera partiellement observable, avec jusqu’à 94,9 % du Soleil masqué à Guéret. Le maximum de l’éclipse aura lieu en soirée, près de l’horizon, offrant un spectacle lumineux unique. Les observateurs devront utiliser des lunettes adaptées pour une vision sécurisée.

offbeat.title: PRONOS PARIS RMC Le pari sûr du 22 juillet - Ligue Conférence
offbeat.summary: SC Braga faces FK Železničar Pančevo in the UEFA Europa Conference League second qualifying round on July 22 at 21:00 CET. Braga, unbeaten in five pre-season matches, starts as heavy favorite after opening their domestic league with a win. Železničar, fourth in Serbia’s 2025-26 league, gains an early-season edge with a competitive start. Braga is expected to advance despite Serbian preparation gains.

The PP demanded an apology, calling her comparison unacceptable. Morant warned of laws targeting citizens and urged the PP to condemn hate speech. The dispute escalated after her TVE remarks on Wednesday.

offbeat.title: Charli XCX recrute Vincent Cassel dans le clip de son nouveau single "Camera"
offbeat.summary: Charli XCX’s new single Camera, from her album Music, Fashion, Film out July 24, features an unexpected cameo by Vincent Cassel in its music video. Filmed in black and white, the clip shows Cassel attempting a desert shoot scene that repeatedly fails due to technical mishaps, including a misfiring explosive and a flaming projector. Frustrated, he mimics a gun to his temple—a nod to his iconic role in La Haine—before storming off set. The clip blends high-energy pop with cinematic chaos ahead of the album’s release.

The 22-year-old Argentina international has completed a medical in Birmingham and has a pre-agreed four-year contract at Villa Park if the loan becomes permanent. The obligation to buy clause is expected to be triggered based on appearances next season. Villa have made four signings this summer, while Chelsea have recouped nearly £200m from player sales.

offbeat.summary: Heavy rain and localized thunderstorms hit the Marche region Friday afternoon, prompting a 36-hour yellow weather alert from 2 p.m. Friday to midnight Sunday. The worst-hit areas included the Pesarese sector, with towns like Piandimeleto, Mercatale and Peglio reporting intense downpours. Brief coastal and hillside showers are expected overnight Saturday into Sunday morning before conditions stabilize with dry, sunny weather through Monday.

offbeat.summary: A filha de Elize Matsunaga, Helena, de 16 anos, vive com os avós paternos desde o assassinato de seu pai, Marcos Kitano Matsunaga, em 2012. A Justiça mantém Elize sem autorização para visitas ou comunicação direta com a filha. Em 2021, durante uma saída temporária, Elize exibiu um cartaz à adolescente com a mensagem "Minha filha, te amarei além da vida". A relação entre mãe e filha poderá ser reavaliada quando Helena atingir a maioridade.

offbeat.title: Televisión Canaria presenta ‘Venezuela: un mes bajo los escombros’
offbeat.summary: Televisión Canaria presents a special report tonight at 21:30 analyzing the humanitarian crisis after Venezuela’s double earthquake on June 24. The disaster killed over 5,000 people, injured thousands, and destroyed or severely damaged nearly 18,000 homes. The program marks one month since the quake, focusing on recovery efforts in La Guaira and Caracas. A local team documented the emergency on the ground over the past weeks.

offbeat.title: Accidental death recorded at inquest of Kerry teenager
offbeat.summary: A jury has returned a verdict of accidental death for 14-year-old Thomas Healy, who died when the car he was driving crashed into a tree on Ross Road, Killarney, on July 7, 2021. The car was being pursued by a Garda van after leaving a Killarney shopping centre car park. His 15-year-old passenger, who was severely injured, told the inquest they had been trying to calm each other before the crash. A 2022 Garda investigation cleared the pursuing officer of any wrongdoing.

offbeat.title: Islande: vague d'indignation dans le pays après le saccage d'un site naturel par un touriste français
offbeat.summary: A French tourist destroyed protected landscapes in Iceland’s central Highlands in June by driving two Land Rovers off-route, posting videos online. Local authorities fined him 300,000 ISK (about €3,000), but he continued the damage. Police later arrested him again for the same offense. Iceland’s government faces growing criticism for its response to the incident.

offbeat.title: He spotted a sports relic in the thrift store. It’s about to make him rich.
offbeat.summary: A 59-year-old hiker died after being struck by lightning on Utah’s American Fork Twin Peaks Saturday. Craig Nelson collapsed near the summit while descending with his brother as storm clouds rolled in. Rescue teams waited for the storm to pass before retrieving his body. Authorities continue investigating while awaiting autopsy results.

offbeat.title: Il processo di stasera a "Un giorno in pretura"
offbeat.summary: The trial of Giampiero Gualandi, accused of killing Sofia Stefani, concludes tonight on Un giorno in pretura at 9:15 PM on Rai 3. The 64-year-old former police commander allegedly shot the 33-year-old vigilessa in Anzola dell’Emilia on May 16, 2024, over a volatile relationship. Prosecutors claim he feared exposure of their affair, while the defense argues Stefani provoked the fatal shooting. The case centers on disputed accounts of their troubled romance.

offbeat.title: Cameron Menzies became unwell during his second-round match against Ross Smith
offbeat.summary: Cameron Menzies’ World Matchplay darts match against Ross Smith was abandoned Wednesday night after he became dizzy and fainted at Blackpool’s Winter Gardens. The 37-year-old Scot, leading 2-1, was treated for high blood pressure and later returned to his accommodation. The second-round clash will not resume. Later sessions feature Gian van Veen and Gerwyn Price as they vie for quarter-final spots.

offbeat.title: Ola de calor en España: ahora llega el punto álgido con hasta 44ºC
offbeat.summary: Spain braces for peak heatwave temperatures of up to 44°C between Wednesday and Thursday, with red alerts issued for Murcia, Alicante and Valencia. The interior, Andalusia and Madrid will see widespread highs above 40°C, while Mediterranean regions get slight relief from coastal breezes. Forecasters warn the heat will intensify before easing over the weekend.

offbeat.title: Gratteri bacchetta Salvini sul caso Roggero: “La politica si dia una calmata e la smetta di aizzare la gente”. Su La7
offbeat.summary: Nicola Gratteri, procuratore capo di Napoli, ha criticato duramente la politica per aver strumentalizzato due casi giudiziari: gli scontri di piazza a Bologna e la condanna del gioielliere Mario Roggero. Gratteri ha definito le polemiche "propaganda elettorale" e ha invitato a rispettare le sentenze definitive, sottolineando che la grazia può essere concessa solo dopo un percorso di ravvedimento in carcere. Ha esortato a evitare processi mediatici, affidando la valutazione ai magistrati e agli educatori penitenziari.

offbeat.title: Watch Maidenhead friendly on Wolves TV | Men's First-Team | News
offbeat.summary: Wolves face Maidenhead United in their first pre-season friendly under new head coach Cesar Peixoto this Wednesday, July 22, at York Road. The National League South side hosts the Premier League club in a landmark match tied to a past transfer deal. Fans can watch the 7.30pm kickoff for free on Wolves TV with full commentary. The fixture is part of a four-game pre-season schedule, with three subsequent matches priced at £5 each.

offbeat.title: La Stazione spaziale internazionale attraversa i cieli: passaggio visibile anche nel Bresciano. Ecco quando
offbeat.summary: Tonight, July 18, the International Space Station will be visible over Brescia at 11:08 PM. The five-minute pass will appear as a bright, steady light moving east-northeast. No equipment is needed if skies are clear. Check Heavens-Above for exact timing by location.

offbeat.title: [Galaxy Unpacked July 2026] A First Look at Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra, Galaxy Z Fold8 and Galaxy Z Flip8
offbeat.summary: Samsung unveiled three new foldable smartphones at Galaxy Unpacked in London on July 2026. The lineup includes the first Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra with an 8-inch foldable display and a 4.1mm thin profile when unfolded. The series also features the Galaxy Z Fold8 and Galaxy Z Flip8, both enhanced with AI-driven features. Shipments are expected to begin next month.

offbeat.title: EastEnders kicks off Linda Carter romance story as new character arrives
offbeat.summary: A new romance is set to unfold in EastEnders as Linda Carter sparks up a connection with councillor Clive Masters, played by Jason Durr. The pair’s chemistry unfolds during a salsa class at Walford’s community centre, where Linda rallies locals to save the venue. Scenes airing next week show their instant attraction after Ian arranges a meeting. The storyline follows Linda’s fight to keep the centre open, bringing Clive into her orbit.

offbeat.title: Gespierde armen, krachtige taal: Van Persie en Steijn beloven 'heel veel goals'
offbeat.summary: Feyenoord’s training camp in Belgium features 32 players, including returning striker Shaqueel van Persie and Sem Steijn, both recovering from knee injuries. Van Persie aims to reclaim the starting striker role and score consistently under new coach Giovanni van Bronckhorst. Steijn, also injury-hit, highlights off-field conditioning as key to his comeback. The squad’s physical focus signals high expectations for the upcoming season.

offbeat.title: €1.5 billion for tax cuts in Budget - Economic Statement
offbeat.summary: €1.5 billion in tax cuts is planned for October’s Budget, as outlined in the Summer Economic Statement. The Government also proposes €7 billion in spending increases next year, including €5.9 billion for day-to-day costs and €1.1 billion for infrastructure like energy and transport. Total expenditure will rise to €125 billion in 2027, a 5.9% increase on 2026. The statement warns of caution over reliance on corporation tax revenue.

offbeat.title: Toter Wal vom Badestrand entfernt - Teile des Kopfes fehlten
offbeat.summary: A dead porpoise washed ashore on Norderney’s popular beach was removed Tuesday after parts of its head were missing. Authorities confirmed the carcass was taken to disposal following reports Monday near a tourist bar. A second dead porpoise found two weeks ago was sent for examination on the mainland. Inspectors could not analyze the latest remains due to severe decomposition.

offbeat.title: Police formally investigate woman after Brit stabbed to death in French village
offbeat.summary: A 70-year-old woman has been formally investigated in connection with the 2023 stabbing death of 65-year-old British-South African mother Karen Carter in Trémolat, France. Carter was found with stab wounds near her car by a friend in the Dordogne village where she lived and managed two holiday rental homes. The woman was initially taken into custody in 2025 as part of preliminary inquiries before being released. She will now face a trial decision after the investigating judge found sufficient evidence.

offbeat.title: Trump threatens to target Iran's bridges and power plants if Hormuz attacks persist
offbeat.summary: US President Donald Trump has threatened to destroy Iranian bridges and power plants if attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz continue. The US has launched strikes on Iran for the 11th consecutive night, prompting Tehran to activate air defences in the capital. Trump's threat specifically targets structures located next to or in Tehran. Iran has warned of reprisals after the US said it would soon hit an underground nuclear site near Natanz.

offbeat.title: Nie żyje piosenkarz Ryszard Pisarski. "Wielki smutek i szok"
offbeat.summary: Popularny w czasach PRL piosenkarz Ryszard Pisarski zmarł 21 lipca 2026 roku w wieku 87 lat. Informację o jego śmierci podał dziennikarz Maciej Klociński, który dzień wcześniej rozmawiał z artystą. Przyczyna zgonu nie została ujawniona. Rodzina i fani składają kondolencje.

offbeat.title: Tennis: Ana Ivanović macht ein trauriges Geständnis
offbeat.summary: Ana Ivanović revealed she retired from tennis in December 2016 due to severe mental health struggles, including panic attacks during matches. The former French Open champion and world No. 1 spoke openly about her decision for the first time in a July 2026 interview. Ivanović, now 38, cited injuries and mental health as key factors in her early exit at age 29. She described debilitating panic attacks triggered by stepping onto the court.

offbeat.title: The Hawk review – Will Ferrell’s dated golf comedy just isn’t that funny
offbeat.summary: Will Ferrell stars in a new Netflix golf comedy, his first scripted series since 2019. The show premiered on April 18, 2025, marking his return to broad comedy after recent dramatic roles. Reviews call it a throwback to early 2000s slapstick, testing whether Ferrell’s style still resonates. Critics note the series leans into the same brand of exaggerated humor that defined his breakout films.

offbeat.title: Meldungen - Schlag gegen eine der weltweit gefährlichsten Phishing-Gruppierungen
offbeat.summary: German and U.S. authorities dismantled the Kratos phishing service on July 20, 2026, seizing over 200 servers and arresting the Indonesian developer. The tool helped criminals create fake Microsoft login pages to steal credentials from 850 victims across 35 countries. Authorities say Kratos operated as a malware-as-a-service platform, rented to other cybercriminals. Investigations continue into the broader network of users.

offbeat.title: „Mir wurde die Hose runtergezogen und zwischen die Beine gefasst“
offbeat.summary: Bill Kaulitz, 36, frontman of Tokio Hotel, revealed he faced queerphobic assaults as a teenager, including having his pants pulled down and being groped in a swimming pool. The attacks aimed to question his gender identity, with Kaulitz calling the incidents nearly routine and keeping them secret out of shame. In his 2021 memoir, he first documented these experiences. Kaulitz now advocates for greater acceptance in the music industry.

offbeat.title: The Odyssey film fans inspired to go back to the source
offbeat.summary: The Odyssey film grossed £12.6m in the UK, making it the second-biggest opening weekend of the year behind Toy Story 5. Shot entirely with Imax 70mm cameras, it set a UK record for highest Imax opening weekend takings. Only three UK screens—London’s BFI and Science Museum, and Manchester’s Printworks—are showing it in 70mm. Screenings cost more but critics call the experience immersive.

offbeat.title: Après les fortes chaleurs, des orages possiblement violents sont attendus… mais jusqu’à quand va durer la canicule dans la région
offbeat.summary: A heatwave gripping southern France’s Languedoc-Roussillon region will persist until Saturday, with temperatures still exceeding 30°C daily. Storms, potentially severe, are forecast to arrive Saturday as conditions shift. Mistral winds are currently driving the heat, but a weather change is expected by the weekend.

offbeat.title: Catherine Keyl schrikt van bizarre tips Tim Hofman: ‘Dit is shocking’
offbeat.summary: Catherine Keyl expressed shock over controversial homework tips in a book by Tim Hofman for the BrainBoost tutoring school. The book allegedly includes advice on cheating, such as using multiple phones to look up answers during tests. Hofman’s publisher confirmed the book’s release, though details remain under scrutiny. Authorities may review the content following public backlash.

offbeat.title: Modelling scout linked to Epstein found dead in Paris
offbeat.summary: A modelling scout linked to Jeffrey Epstein was found dead at his Paris home on Monday. Daniel Siad, 69, was discovered in Colombes, a northwest suburb, and authorities opened an investigation into his death. Siad’s name appeared thousands of times in released Epstein files, though he denied knowledge of Epstein’s crimes. French prosecutors had previously investigated him for alleged human trafficking and conspiracy but closed the case in February due to insufficient evidence.

offbeat.title: NCIS Reveals First-Ever Recast Character in 31 Years in New Set Video
offbeat.summary: NCIS will recast a character for the first time in its 31-year history, as revealed in a new set video. The change comes as the long-running series prepares for its upcoming season. Details on the recast or affected character have not been disclosed. The shift marks a significant milestone for the iconic procedural.

offbeat.title: RETE 4
offbeat.summary: A 90-year-old Illinois florist turned drug courier, Earl Stone, faces prison after a 2016 bust. The film The Mule recounts his 2016-2018 run transporting cocaine for a cartel. Based on a true story, the movie premieres December 14. Eastwood stars as Stone, reflecting on family and regret.

offbeat.title: It's Officially the End of an Era for Marvel's 'Wonder Man'
offbeat.summary: It's 2024: Marvel's "Wonder Man" series has been canceled after one season. The show starred Yahya Abdul-Mateen II as the titular hero, set in the MCU. Production wrapped in late 2023. Fans can expect a finale resolving key storylines next year.

tech.title: Price-hiked iPads are a little cheaper right now
tech.summary: A premium 13-inch iPad Pro with 512GB of storage is currently discounted by $150 at Amazon and $100 at Best Buy, bringing its price down to $1,549.99. This high-end iPad features Apple's M5 processor, an OLED screen, and support for the Apple Pencil Pro and Magic Keyboard. The deal is available on the 2025 model, which normally costs $1,699. Those looking for a more affordable option may also consider the discounted 11-inch iPad Air M3 with 1TB of storage, now priced at $749.

tech.title: iOS code could reportedly let Apple cut off apps when users miss iPhone payments
tech.summary: Apple's iOS 27 beta code reveals a new "Restricted Mode" that could be triggered if users miss iPhone payments, restricting access to nearly all apps except for basic functions. This feature is part of a new financing program, "Apple Upgrade," which allows users to lease new devices. The code also includes a "Partner Finance Lock" to prevent device erasure or resale. If implemented, this feature would impact users who fail to make payments on financed iPhones.

tech.title: Teenager drops social media addiction lawsuit against Meta
tech.summary: A 15-year-old Florida boy has dropped his lawsuit against Meta, citing concerns about a lengthy jury trial, after previously settling similar claims against TikTok, Snapchat, and YouTube. The case, which aimed to hold social media companies accountable for allegedly creating addictive platforms, is now closed. Meta claims the lawsuit was baseless. The teenager will focus on his recovery and therapy.

entertainment.title: ‘Disney Worldbuilders’ Documentary by Leslie Iwerks, Featuring James Cameron, Kevin Feige and Jon Favreau, Set to Premiere on Disney+
entertainment.summary: A new documentary, Disney Worldbuilders, premieres on Disney+ August 20, offering a behind-the-scenes look at how Disney turns films like Frozen and Zootopia into theme park attractions. Directed by Leslie Iwerks, the film features interviews with James Cameron, Kevin Feige, and Jon Favreau. The documentary traces the creative process from screen to park, showcasing the collaboration behind immersive Disney worlds. Viewers can expect exclusive insights into the making of these attractions.

entertainment.title: ‘EA Sports FC’ Sets World Cup Recordbreaker Kylian Mbappé as New Cover Athlete (Gaming News Roundup)
entertainment.summary: Kylian Mbappé will grace the cover of EA Sports FC 27’s Ultimate Edition and mobile version, marking his first cover appearance since 2020. The French striker, now at Real Madrid, set a World Cup goals record with his 22nd goal in the latest tournament. EA Sports calls him a defining player of his generation. The game launches this fall.

entertainment.title: Bose Wants to Be the Centerpiece of Your Living Room Again With the Lifestyle Ultra Speaker
entertainment.summary: Bose has relaunched its Lifestyle Ultra Speaker, priced at $299, as the flagship wireless speaker in its revived Lifestyle Collection. The new model, available in Black and White Smoke, is a bestseller on Best Buy and joins the Lifestyle Ultra Soundbar and Subwoofer. Customers can start with one speaker and expand into a multi-room setup or full home theater. A Best Buy bundle with the soundbar and subwoofer is offered at $1,699.

politics.title: Burnham stirs up political row with review of release plans for violent criminals
politics.summary: Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham has ordered a review of plans to release thousands of violent offenders from prison early, affecting around 6,000 individuals. The move comes after the family of PC Andrew Harper expressed outrage over the potential release of his killers. The review aims to address concerns over public safety and the criminal justice system. The outcome of the review will likely influence the government's decision on the early release of these offenders.

politics.title: Tories ask HMRC to investigate whether Nigel Farage owes tax on £5m gift
politics.summary: The Conservatives have asked HMRC to investigate whether Nigel Farage owes tax on a £5m gift from crypto billionaire Christopher Harborne. Farage claims the money was a personal gift with no conditions, while the Tories suggest it may have been compensation for political activities. HMRC has not yet confirmed if an investigation will proceed. Farage has previously stated the funds were for security and Brexit campaigning.

politics.title: Laredo’s Future Rides on Trump’s Next Trade Move
politics.summary: The economy of Laredo, Texas, has boomed under the president’s global tariffs. Now, his demands may threaten its success.

facts.title: NASA to Showcase Agency’s Newest Wind Tunnel in Virginia
facts.summary: NASA will showcase its newest wind tunnel, the Flight Dynamics Research Facility, at the Langley Research Center in Hampton, Virginia, on July 31. The facility, the agency's first new wind tunnel in over 40 years, will be open to a media tour and ribbon-cutting ceremony. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman and other officials will be available for media questions. The event marks a significant milestone in NASA's evolution and will provide insight into the agency's future research capabilities.

sports.title: Rangers to fight McInnes ban for 'disgusting' penalty remark
sports.summary: Rangers manager Derek McInnes has been given a four-match touchline ban by the Scottish FA for calling a late penalty decision "disgusting". The penalty, awarded to Celtic in a 3-2 win at Motherwell in May, was a stoppage-time spot-kick that kept Celtic one point behind Hearts. McInnes is contesting the ban, claiming his comments were directed at the decision, not officials. He will serve the first three games of the ban, with the fourth suspended until the end of the season.

sports.title: Inter Miami under investigation after signing Casemiro
sports.summary: Major League Soccer is investigating Inter Miami for alleged tampering after the club signed Brazilian midfielder Casemiro on a deal through 2027 with an option to extend until 2029. The investigation concerns Casemiro's discovery rights, which Los Angeles Galaxy held before Inter Miami acquired them. The league is gathering information and will comment once the review is complete. A settlement between Inter Miami and the LA Galaxy for Casemiro's rights is pending the outcome of the investigation.

breaking.title: Dubai Firm Cuts Deal for New Port to Bypass Strait of Hormuz
breaking.summary: A Dubai-based logistics giant unveiled a deal with the United Arab Emirates on Wednesday to develop a new port system that would bypass the Strait of Hormuz.

breaking.title: Israeli Banks Threaten to Cut Off Palestinian Economy
breaking.summary: Two Israeli financial firms have long served as partners to Palestinian banks, handling transactions that make possible the West Bank’s imports and exports.

breaking.title: Hitler’s Birthplace in Austria Is Now a Police Station
breaking.summary: The government repurposed the 17th century building in hopes of preventing the site from becoming an attraction for Nazi sympathizers.

crisis.title: Sony Revives Cinerama Dome in Los Angeles as Box Office Sales Rebound
crisis.summary: The iconic Cinerama Dome and adjacent 14-screen theater complex will reopen in 2028 after a renovation. The venue closed in 2020 because of the pandemic.

crisis.title: Trump’s Trade Negotiator Doubles Down on Tariffs Before Global Duties Expire
crisis.summary: Jamieson Greer, the president’s top trade negotiator, told the Senate that the United States remained intent on tariffs as it prepared to replace duties the Supreme Court struck down.

crisis.title: Global Oil Price Jumps as War Threats Intensify
crisis.summary: But stock investors shrug off Iran developments as they await crucial earnings reports from tech firms including Alphabet and Tesla.

health.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
health.summary: A British health committee report finds that ultra-processed foods contribute to obesity, with sugar and refined carbohydrates being primary drivers. The report recommends targeting these specific nutrients in food policy. The committee's findings are based on evidence showing that fat and salt are not the main culprits in weight gain and type 2 diabetes. The UK government is now under pressure to revise its food labeling and reformulation targets accordingly.

health.title: Fifth person dies in New York City amid legionnaires’ disease outbreak
health.summary: A fifth person has died in New York City’s legionnaires’ disease outbreak, bringing the toll to five fatalities among 82 confirmed cases. Health officials traced the outbreak to cooling towers on Manhattan’s Upper East Side, where 34 tested positive for legionella bacteria. Eight patients remain hospitalized, while 56 have recovered and been discharged. No new cases have been reported in six days, but officials warn figures may still change as testing continues.

health.title: Government Overhauls Teen Pregnancy Program to Focus on Marriage and Starting Families
health.summary: The administration canceled federal grants to organizations across the country, accusing them of encouraging premarital sex. New criteria say programs should stress abstinence.
